title: Improvements for calling functions with empty arguments
body:
SystemVerilog allows to call a function with empty arguments if the function specifies a default value. Address a few small issues around this.

1) If the same function is invoked multiple time and using default port values, each invocation ends up with the same NetExpr. This results in undefined behavior as there are now multiple owners of that NetExpr. To avoid this duplicate the default port value each time it is used.

2) There are two places that do not account for empty function arguments, resulting in a NULL pointer deref. This happens for detecting implicit nets in function call arguments and detection automatic terms in function call arguments.

3) Trailing empty arguments are pruned from function and task calls. This means no error is reported when too many empty arguments are specified. Modify this to only prune the argument list, if there is exactly one empty argument. The parser produces an argument list with exactly one empty argument when it encounters an empty argument list, e.g. `t();`. There was code for handling this in many different places and now has been centralized in a single function in the parser.
